steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence uses a not only [blank] but also illuminate structure. Illuminate means to shed light on or clarify something. The blank must be a verb that is parallel in meaning—something about perceiving or recognizing. The subject doing this action is paleoanthropologists working with fossil remains.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'discern': means to perceive or recognize clearly. Parallels illuminate by conveying the act of gaining understanding. ✓
  • Choice B — 'yield': means to produce or give way. The fossil remains are not “yielding” steps in evolution—that reverses the logical direction. ✗
  • Choice C — 'exploit': means to make use of, often with a negative connotation. Paleoanthropologists do not “exploit” steps in evolution; the word is both tonally and semantically off. ✗
  • Choice D — 'prioritize': means to rank in order of importance. Nothing in the passage suggests ranking steps against each other. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
